concerns.text: FR-002 (motion exclusion >3mm) and FR-010 (condition verification) lack a dedicated task to generate the final `subjects_metadata.json` list of retained subjects used by downstream phases. T017 outputs this file but depends on T014 (rejected) and T016 (marked complete but unverified). The chain to produce the definitive list of N subjects for US2/US3 is broken.

FR-003 (ROI extraction) and FR-004 (segmentation) are covered by T024 and T025, but FR-005 (Mean Absolute Correlation) is split between T027 (calculation) and T028 (saving). T028 is marked missing. Without T028, the `connectivity_metrics.json` artifact required for US3 (FR-006) is not produced, breaking the data flow.

FR-007 (associational framing) and FR-008 (FDR correction) are partially covered by T034 and T037, but the final report generation (T039) is marked missing. T039 is the only task responsible for injecting the 'associational' string into the final report and aggregating FDR results. Without it, FR-007 and FR-008 are not fully satisfied in the output artifact.

Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) requires updating `state/projects/...yaml` with artifact hashes. T007 (utility) is marked missing. While T012, T014, T028, and T039 call this utility, the utility itself is not implemented. This breaks the checksum flow for all data artifacts, violating the Constitution.

T016 is marked `[X]` (complete) but T014 (which depends on it) is marked missing/rejected. This creates a logical inconsistency in the task state. If T016 is complete, its output should exist; if it doesn't, T016 should not be marked complete. This is a documentation/stale tag issue.
